previous_statement: (Hypotheses verification — CORRECTED scope). The main theorem applies to: (A) C*-algebras, (B) Archimedean order unit spaces, and (C) *-algebras equipped with an Archimedean quadratic module M (following Klep-Magron-Volcic / the formally verified framework in af-tests). Specifically: (i) For C*-algebras A and states phi in S(A), the GNS construction yields bounded representations (by the C*-identity ||a*a|| = ||a||^2 giving ||pi(a)|| <= ||a||), so the face-commutant correspondence (Node 1.1) applies. (ii) For *-algebras with Archimedean quadratic module M, M-positive states are uniformly bounded (by Cauchy-Schwarz + Archimedean property: |phi(a)| <= sqrt(N_a) where a*a <= N_a·1 mod M), the state space S_M is compact in the pointwise topology (by Tychonoff), and the GNS construction yields bounded representations. This extends the scope beyond C*-algebras. (iii) The state space S(A) (or S_M) is compact convex in the weak-* (pointwise) topology. Compactness follows from Banach-Alaoglu for C*-algebras, or from Tychonoff + closedness of state conditions for Archimedean *-algebras. (iv) The functionals f_j(psi) = psi(a_j) are continuous affine on S(A) by definition of the weak-* topology (not because a_j is 'bounded' per se). The Weis-Shirokov theorem (Node 1.2) applies with K = S(A) and m constraints. Note: for GENERAL *-algebras without the Archimedean property, the GNS construction may give unbounded operators and the state space need not be compact, so the theorem does not apply in that generality.
new_statement:
(Hypotheses verification — CORRECTED scope, 2nd amendment). The main theorem applies to: (A) C*-algebras, (B) Archimedean order unit spaces (V, V^+, e) with order-unit norm, and (C) *-algebras equipped with an Archimedean quadratic module M. For each case, we verify the hypotheses of Nodes 1.1 (face-commutant) and 1.2 (Weis-Shirokov). This node depends on Nodes 1.1 and 1.2.

(i) GNS gives BOUNDED representations: For C*-algebras, the C*-identity ||a*a|| = ||a||² gives ||π(a)|| ≤ ||a||. For Archimedean order unit spaces, the order-unit norm provides boundedness. For *-algebras with Archimedean quadratic module M, M-positive states are uniformly bounded (Cauchy-Schwarz + Archimedean: |φ(a)| ≤ √N_a where a*a ≤ N_a·1 mod M). In all three cases, the GNS representation is bounded, so the commutant π_φ(A)' = {T ∈ B(H) : Tπ(a) = π(a)T ∀a} is a well-defined von Neumann algebra, and the face-commutant correspondence (Node 1.1) applies.

(ii) Compactness of K = S(A): For C*-algebras, states lie in the unit ball of A* which is weak-* compact by Banach-Alaoglu. For Archimedean order unit spaces, the order-unit norm gives a Banach space structure and Banach-Alaoglu applies. For *-algebras with Archimedean quadratic module, S_M is compact in the pointwise topology by Tychonoff's theorem (S_M embeds as a closed subset of a product of compact intervals, using the Archimedean bounds). In all cases K = S(A) (or S_M) is compact convex.

(iii) Continuity of constraint functionals: The functionals f_j(ψ) = ψ(a_j) are continuous affine on S(A) by DEFINITION of the weak-* (or pointwise) topology — evaluation at a fixed element is continuous by the very definition of this topology. This holds regardless of whether A has a norm. The Weis-Shirokov theorem (Node 1.2) applies with K = S(A) and m constraints.

Note: For GENERAL *-algebras without the Archimedean property, the GNS construction may give unbounded operators, the commutant is not well-defined in the standard sense, and the state space need not be compact. The theorem does not apply in that generality. This node is a verification/deduction step, not an assumption.
